name: In This Moment
bio:
Nature counts the black widow spider amongst its most fascinating and dangerous anomalies. The female arachnid often devours its mate after copulation. It's both a delicate and dangerous predator. In This Moment isn't all that different from this enigmatic beast. Led by frontwoman Maria Brink, the Los Angeles hard rock outfit strikes with a seductive metallic bite on their aptly titled fifth full-length album, Black Widow.

"Black Widow is a metaphor for this innocent young girl who gets infected with life, traumas, experiences, and the balance of light and darkness," explains Maria. "She becomes this poised and powerful creature. That's the album."

"We went into this with the title Black Widow," says lead guitarist Chris Howorth. "It fits the image of Maria as this powerful heroine. If you think of the boys in the audience watching our stage show, she's like the black widow pulling them all in."

The record, the first for the band on Atlantic Records, picks up where the group's 2012 breakthrough, Blood, left off. That album saw In This Moment debut at #15 on the Billboard Top 200, their highest chart position to date, and eventually sold over 250,000 units in the U.S. alone. It also spawned the single "Blood," which rose to #9 on the Mainstream Rock and Active Rock Songs charts. A sold out headline tour, HELLPOP, followed, as well as appearances at the Uproar Festival and Rock On The Range, and jaunts with Shinedown and Papa Roach. After the whirlwind of Blood subsided, Maria and Chris retreated to Las Vegas in February 2014 to begin working on what would become Black Widow with longtime producer Kevin Churko [Ozzy Osbourne, Five Finger Death Punch].

name: indigo
bio:
Electric Indigo works as musician, composer and DJ. Her name stands for the intelligent interpretation of techno and electronic music. 

She started her DJ career in 1989 in Vienna and worked at the legendary Hard Wax record store in Berlin from 1993 to 1996. In 1998, she created female:pressure, the international database for female, non-binary and transgender artists in the fields of electronic music and digital arts which got an Honorary Mention at Prix Ars Electronica 2009. 2012 she received the 'oustanding artist award' in the category Computer Music from the Austrian Ministry for Arts and Culture. 

She makes music for concert spaces, clubs and occasionally for stage plays and short films. In her compositions and live performances, she emphasizes the spatial-temporal placement of subtly elaborated sounds and structures, often generated from speech recordings. Electric Indigo shuttles between Vienna and Berlin.

name: Infinity Song
bio:
Infinity Song is a New York City-based soft rock band made up of four siblings—Abraham, Angel, Israel, and Momo Boyd. Known for their tight harmonies, dreamy lyrics, and sublime guitar riffs, they create a transcendent musical experience both live and in the studio.

Homeschooled academically and musically, alongside five other siblings, they were raised on classical, gospel, and jazz influences like Pat Metheny, Marvin Gaye, and The Winans Family. Their journey began when their father, John Boyd, moved the family from Detroit to NYC in 2006, where they performed everywhere—from the Metropolitan Museum of Art to Times Square, eventually making Bethesda Fountain in Central Park their home stage for 12 years.

After building a loyal fan base, the group caught the attention of JAY Z, who signed them to Roc Nation in 2016, advising them to stay true to their unique artistry. Their 2020 debut album Mad Love and viral videos earned them millions of views and celebrity endorsements. Now, with their latest EP METAMORPHOSIS and hit singles Haters Anthem and Slow Burn, Infinity Song is drawing comparisons to legendary ‘70s groups like Fifth Dimension and ABBA. With momentum building, they’re poised to leave an indelible mark on today’s music culture.

name: INGUERZON
bio:
Inguerzon is a Chilean Drum & Bass DJ and producer with more than a decade embedded in the global electronic circuit. Rising from Chile’s underground and later spending six formative years in Australia, he sharpened his craft within a culture deeply connected to UK-rooted Drum & Bass — shaping a sound that fuses Latin intensity with authentic British influence.

His performance history spans major international stages including Creamfields (2016, 2017, 2022 x2, 2025), Ultra Music Festival (2016), Dreambeach (2019), Mysteryland (2013), UKF Argentina (2016) and Marquee Las Vegas (2017). Over the years, he has shared bills with — and supported — some of the most respected names in the game: S.P.Y, Sigma, Bryan Gee, General Levy, Original Sin, Sub Zero, Mrs Magoo, Deekline, Klue, Mampi Swift, Ed Solo, Benny Page, Level 2, Alix Perez, Ed Rush & Optical, Alibi, L-Side, Diplo, Roger Sanchez, Nervo and Dimitri Vegas & Like Mike, among others.

Renowned for tight technical execution and forward-thinking programming, Inguerzon’s sets move effortlessly through the full Drum & Bass spectrum — from atmospheric textures to rolling low-end pressure and uncompromising peak-time energy. His approach is deliberate and narrative-led, favouring tension, release and flow over quick-fire selection.

Now based in Chile, he stands at the forefront of the country’s Drum & Bass resurgence as founder of Trigga Fingaz — a platform born in Australia and now driving the genre’s expansion in Santiago. Through carefully curated events, grassroots community building and new productions targeted at the UK market, Inguerzon is positioning himself not just as a DJ, but as a key figure in the next phase of South America’s Drum & Bass evolution, with international touring across Australia and England firmly in sight.

name: INVT
bio:
Miami duo INVT are known for their sound system-focused music, blending club genres with dub influences, swung rhythms, and Latin-rooted percussion. 

Their catalog spans over 200 releases, alongside live hardware performances and DJ sets at institutions including DC-10 (Circoloco), Fabric, Panorama Bar, Blitz, Club Space, Nowadays and Draaimolen, with notable b2bs with acts like Skream, Skee Mask, Sammy Virji, Bradley Zero and DJ Python.

Emerging independently during the pandemic, INVT built momentum through a prolific run of self-released music on Bandcamp, quickly earning support from some of the most influential DJs in the world. Sustaining that momentum through consistent releases, the duo became recognized for a high-volume yet high-quality output.

All aspects of the project are developed in house by the pair. They write, produce, mix, and master their own music, while also designing the visual side of the project themselves through clothing collections, art exhibits, videos, and album artwork. 

Built on lifelong friendship and a shared commitment to authentic electronic music, INVT continue to champion Miami talent while carving out their own lane within contemporary global dance music.  



contact us: innovatemiami305@gmail.com

name: IPeiqi
bio:
🇨🇳Chinese Producer
🎵House/Break
🅰️Anjunadeep
😺Monstercat Silk

IPeiqi, a Melodic House/Techno music producer from China, has already made a name for himself with his eight years of deep experience in electronic music production. He is the first Chinese musician to release works on Anjunadeep and Monstercat Silk labels, and has also released songs on Songspire, a music label owned by Blackhole, as well as influential domestic music labels such as AROS, Prajnasonic, Ran Music, and Deepfaction. His works are unique and break the traditional framework of Progressive House. He follows his inner feelings, with soul melodies and beautiful atmosphere as the core, creating music with a unique gentle and warm temperament. In recent years, IPeiqi has collaborated with Anjunadeep and Anjuna China on several tour performances, including the highly anticipated 2024 Creamfields Hong Kong and Zouk Singapore shows. The contagious stage atmosphere he brought was deeply loved by the audience and won wide acclaim.

Bio

Iris Menza is a respected Belgian DJ who plays a sophisticated blend of dub-tech and deep house. She is known for her captivating performances at Festivals and clubs such as Amsterdam Open Air, Frieda’s Buxe Zurich, Suicide club Berlin and Fuse Brussels.
 
Iris grew up listening to 80s music and 90s hip hop close to the Dutch border in a small Belgian village called Maasmechelen. At 17 she discovered house music and immediately felt a deep connection to the sound. After spending some years on the dancefloor, she purchased turntables and became an eclectic collector of timeless vinyl.
 
After many years of playing alongside the likes of Chris Stussy, Prunk, Archie Hamilton and Ferro, Iris has become a top representative of Belgian Underground culture. Her music brought her to The Netherlands, Germany, Switzerland and Ibiza. She secured residencies with Deep In You and PIV and has releases out on PIV and Cachai.
 
According to the Belgian DJ, sharing her passion for groovy music with a crowd is the greatest satisfaction achievable. Make sure to share that feeling with her when you get the chance.

name: Isaiah Rashad
bio:
Isaiah Rashad doesn’t look back. Instead, he never stops moving forward. That’s why the internet has tried so hard to keep up since the Chattanooga, TN-born and Los Angeles-based artist emerged in 2012. After one seismic appearance after another throughout 2013, he formally introduced himself with 2014’s Cilvia Demo. XXL named him among its prestigious “Freshman Class,” while the project earned a rare score of 8.2 from Pitchfork. Just two years later, his full-length debut, The Sun’s Tirade, bowed in the Top 20 of the Billboard Top 200 and closed out 2016 on over a dozen year-end lists, including UPROXX, L.A. Weekly, Inverse, Stereogum, Pop Matters, Pigeons & Planes, Pitchfork, and more. In between sold out shows coast-to-coast and racking up nearly 500 million total streams, he quietly stared down his demons out of the spotlight. By the time he dove back into music, life had changed. The process switched up, so he realigned and linked up with new collaborators such as Kenny Beats. Ready to break a five-year creative silence, he returns with his second full-length album The House Is Burning[Top Dawg Entertainment], including the single “Lay Wit Ya” [feat. Duke Deuce]. While the house is burning, Isaiah’s running forward at full steam once again. Try to catch up.

send beats to : itslacasa@gmail.com

name: J Balvin
bio:
J Balvin is the stage name of José Álvaro Osorio Balvín, from Medellín, Colombia. Balvin is a reggaeton singer whose polished urban singles and best-selling albums have made him a top-tier Latin pop artist. He became interested in music at a young age while listening to rap, champeta, bachata, and reggae on the radio. He was also a big fan of music videos; it was from these that he began to develop his dance moves and cultivate his own style.

He signed to EMI Colombia in 2009 and released his first single, "Ella Me Cautivó," which charted at 35 on the U.S. tropical chart. This was followed by the release of the full-length Real in 2010. Balvin contributed four tunes to the soundtrack of the Tim Bulot film El Negocio. All of them charted, including "En Lo Oscuro," which hit the top spot in his native country.

La FamiliaBalvin was a featured vocalist on the Colombian version of Robin Thicke's smash single "Blurred Lines," which was issued in October of 2013, just prior to his own second album, La Familia. All four singles from the album charted in Colombia's Top Ten, and "Yo Te Lo Dije" hit number two on the rhythm chart and number nine on the Latin pop chart in the U.S. The B Sides collection appeared in September of 2014, featuring two more radio chart-topping singles in "6 AM" and "Ay Vamos." The latter won a Latin Grammy for Best Urban Song. His 2015 single "Ginza," the offcial pre-release for his next album, entered the Hot Latin Songs chart at number one, topped several others, and was certified platinum.
EnergíaBalvin's collaborations were as notable as his own work that year. He and Julión Álvarez were featured on Juan Gabriel's single "La Frontera." His "Latino Remix" of Justin Bieber's smash "Sorry" scored big for both artists at Latin pop radio and in digital streaming and downloads. The following year, Balvin won the Premios Lo Nuestro Artist of the Year Award and was a featured guest on both Sofia Carson's charting debut single "Love Is the Name" and on Alejandro Sanz's "Cuando Seas Grande" -- from a performance at La Voz -- along with Gloria Trevi and Los Tigres del Norte. In May, he released the single "Bobo," produced by Pharrell Williams, who also appeared on the track along with Mosty and Bia. It topped the Latin songs chart. The full-length, Energia, was produced by Sky and featured additional contributions from Bull Nene, Daddy Yankee, Fuego, Yandel, and Juanes.

name: JACKSON
bio:
JACKSON is a Brazilian DJ, music producer, and creator of the Hydro-House movement – a new House music subgenre characterized by bold grooves and uplifting vibes, designed to ignite highly dopamine-fueled collective experiences on the dance floor.

With a solid career in the entertainment industry, JACKSON combines musicality with a forward-thinking vision, translating cultural and behavioral trends into unique sonic experiences – both in his productions and performances.

Tomorrowland, Hï Ibiza, and the Zurich Street Parade are among his standout shows. His sensitive versatility also frequently places him in line-ups alongside renowned artists such as Carl Cox, Rufüs du Sol, Eric Prydz, Black Coffee and Vintage Culture.

In 2024, JACKSON released his debut album, Chapter 19, featuring 11 tracks and a music-documentary. With a more visceral and abstract approach, the project was presented at the Museum of Image and Sound in São Paulo and praised by critics.

Currently, JACKSON’s production focus is fully directed toward Hydro-House.

Bio:
JADE SOUL is a Rotterdam-based DJ who’s redefining the rhythm of the dance floor with her genre-bending sets and unmistakable soul-driven energy. Deeply rooted in her Turkish, Moluccan, and Philippine heritage, JADE SOUL brings a vibrant fusion of cultures to every performance—whether she’s behind the decks or curating sonic journeys through her radio shows and events.
Known for spinning music strictly "from the soul," she weaves a storytelling-driven experience that resonates with heart, rhythm, and connection. Her seamless blend of genres—ranging from Batida, Baile Funk, Bass, UKG, Bubbling, Jungle, and beyond—creates a powerful narrative that transcends borders and ignites dance floors.

JADE SOUL is not just a DJ; she’s a movement. With her London-based radio show on SUBTLE FM and her own Rotterdam concept SOULSYNC, she’s building platforms that foster meaningful connections and push sonic boundaries.

Already making waves at major festivals like Lowlands, Tomorrowland, Rolling Loud/Woohah, Elrow, and Openair Frauenfeld, JADE SOUL continues to expand her global presence—including a recent debut with Keep Hush. Wherever she plays, expect an immersive experience charged with energy, culture, and raw emotion—the full JADE SOUL experience.

name: James Carter
bio:
Management/All other inquiries
mgmt@jamescarter.com

James Carter, a highly accomplished music producer, artist, label head, and DJ hailing from London.

James’s talent and versatility have been recognized through numerous collaborations, co-productions, and co-writing endeavors. Notably, he played a
pivotal role in crafting one of the biggest dance hits of 2022 - Bad Memories together with Italian DJ trio MEDUZA and singers Elley Duhé & FAST BOY, which soared up the charts and achieved remarkable success worldwide. This sensational track secured top positions, including #1 in the Czech Republic, Romania, and Poland, #2 in Hungary, #3 in the Netherlands and Slovakia, #6 in Austria, #7 in Belgium, #8 in Australia and Croatia, and #9 in the Netherlands.

His remarkable achievements have been acknowledged with gold certifications in Germany and Switzerland, as well as platinum certifications in Austria and Poland.

The impact of his music has transcended boundaries, captivating audiences globally. In the United States, James Carter's music has seen significant recognition, topping the Media Base Dance Airplay Chart at #1 and reaching #14 on the Billboard Dance Chart. Additionally, ‘Bad Memories’ have garnered an impressive 400 million streams across all platforms since July 2022.

With an astonishing 600 million streams on Spotify alone, James Carter's artistic prowess continues to leave an indelible mark on the industry. His single 'Hands in the Fire' became a viral sensation on TikTok, accumulating over 300 million views on the platform and further solidifying his global appeal.

As a testament to his immense popularity, James Carter boasts 11 million monthly listeners on Spotify as of June 2023, solidifying his status as a sought-after artist and producer.

James Carter continues to push the boundaries of electronic music and leave an enduring impact on the global music scene.

name: James Clav
bio:
James Clav is an emerging DJ and producer with an already remarkable journey in the music world. 
Originally hailing from the midlands of Ireland, he took a leap of faith and relocated to the French Alps to pursue his passion for music. Despite only being in the DJing scene for three years, James's talent and dedication have already earned him significant recognition.

One of his young career highlights was performing at Tomorrowland Winter 2023, where he showcased his skills on not one but three stages. The experience further fueled his determination to leave a lasting mark on the electronic dance music scene.

With an unwavering passion for creating and performing music, James Clav remains a promising talent to watch out for. As he continues to grow as an artist, he strives to inspire and connect with audiences worldwide through his unique sound and electrifying performances.

name: Jamles
bio:
OBSCURA RADIO weekly show - send demo to jamles.mgmt@gmail.com

Jamles
A masked innovator in the world of DJing and production, Jamles champions the philosophy that music should be the sole storyteller. For this long-standing artist, it's all about the sound, emotions, and spectacle-crafting performances that resonate deeply and linger long after the music fades.

Firmly rejecting the distractions of modern-day self-promotion, Jamles refuses to focus on promoting travels, hotels, food, or personal life. Instead, they remain faceless, devoting their energy entirely to delivering an unfiltered, immersive musical journey.

Embarking on a fresh chapter, Jamles invites genuine music lovers to connect with the artistry "not the noise around it" and experience a path where the sound truly speaks for itself.

name: Jan Vervloet
bio:
Contact/Bookings:

djjanvervloet@gmail.com

Born in Belgium, Jan Vervloet is already more than 20 years a well-known name in the Belgian dance music scene.

As a producer he released more than 200 trax worldwide including golden records with Fiocco in Belgium , a nr 1 hit in Holland with Scoop and even a top 10 Hit in USA & France with D.H.T.

In 2007 he founded his own record company (The Hype Records) including artists such as Christophe! -
Dizkodude - Steve Slight - V-Roy ...
All releases got into the Belgian Dance Charts and were spinned & broadcasted worldwide by leading DJ's.

2012 looks really promising for Jan : First collaboration with Dutch DJ Johan Gielen gets straight into the Beatport Top 100 and remixes for Dimaro and Dizkodude have just been released !!!

As a DJ he has been a part of the most selected clubs & parties in the Benelux.
His guest-DJ appearances are just too much to mention all (Kokorico Illusion - Reflex - Millenium - Feestpaleis - Metropolis - Beachland - I Love the 90s - Tomorrowland - Last World - Cityparade - House Clubbing - Gigadance - Qontinent - City Parade - Super Sonic Festival - Dance D-Vision…).

After all those years Jan is still one of the most respected and passioned DJ's around who is not afraid to mash up
all styles of Party Music (Electro - Retro - Progressive House - 90's - Trance - Urban - Dirty House ...) because he has only got 1 target : A Dancing Screaming Packed Dancefloor !!!

name: JANA VITILIGO
bio:
JANA VITILIGO is a trailblazing female DJ from Belgium, known for her electrifying sets and authentic approach to nightlife. With a background in managing her own agency, she developed a sharp creative vision and a passion for delivering unforgettable experiences. This natural progression led her to step behind the decks herself, birthing the persona “JANA,” where she takes full control of the night.


JANA VITILIGO’s story is one of empowerment and transformation. After years of hiding her autoimmune condition, she embraced it as a source of strength and inspiration. By accepting herself fully, she redefined her identity and now inspires others to celebrate their uniqueness. This self-reflection gave rise to “JANA VITILIGO,” a symbol of positivity, resilience, and self-love.


Her music journey has taken her to iconic clubs such as La Rocca, Charlatan, Zuco, Sotto’s,.. as well as exclusive summer hotspots like Bar Buddha, Famouz Knokke, Destino and Nikki Beach Ibiza. 

On the festival circuit, she’s rocked stages at Tomorrowland, Hype O Dream, Dreambeats, Fantasia Festival and Blacklight Festival, among many others.


JANA is a regular guest at some of Belgium’s biggest party concepts, including Delicious Deep, Secret Cinema, House Family, Space vs Creamm and Overtime in Amsterdam. Her artistry extends beyond the dance floor with the creation of her party concept Mood in 2019, a platform where she expresses herself through music deeply connected to emotion and people.


As a producer, JANA has made waves on the global stage, releasing several tracks that have garnered recognition worldwide. Her debut track Somebody dropped in 2020 on the HOTFUSS label, marking the beginning of her journey as a producer. 

With each release, she continues to evolve her sound and expand her reach, captivating audiences worldwide.


JANA VITILIGO is more than just a DJ; she’s a force for empowerment, creativity, and connection, paving her own way in the nightlife world while inspiring others to do the same.

Her sound emphasizes careful selection and unconventional programming across extended durations. She represents eclectic DJing within electronic music contexts.

name: JAO
bio:
Kuba Otlowski - has been associated with melodic techno for many years, but the broad spectrum of electronic music has always been in his area of interest. 
Travels to various places, hundreds of hours spent listening to various artists and their sets, and an ever-growing curiosity to discover new melodic and energetic grooves led him to oriental African sounds and prompted him to launch a new music project and the artistic alias JAO.
Positive energy is the feeling he wants to spread during his performances, to give people happiness through music and put them in the best possible mood! 
He draws inspiration from labels such as Madorasindahouse, Moblack, Scorpios Music, All Day I Dream, Keinemusik, Don't Sit on the Furniture, Warung, Wired and Hoomidas. He is the co-founder of the STIMMULUS platform promoting an electronic selection of Polish artists. 

In recent years, he has performed at the most popular venues such as Smolna, Praga Centrum, Tama, Prozak 2.0, Jasna 1, Sfinks700, Klub NIEBO, SQ, Nowa Rezydencja, 1500m2, and has presented his skills among the others at the Audioriver festival. He was a resident of the legendary Nowa Jerozolima club in Warsaw. He shared the stage with world-class artists such as &Me, Adam Port, Reznik, Loco Dice, Dixon, Ame, Damian Lazarus, Fur Coat, Rebolledo, Landikhan, Igor Marijuan, Da Capo, Elif, Anja Schneider  & many more

name: Javi Reina
bio:
Info : info@javireina.com
Booking / Management: dani@deepdelaymanagement.com

Bailongo Records: @bailongo-records


Javi Reina is a reference in Spain’s electronic music scene with also a huge international projection in the last decade. His technique and musical selection, always at the forefront, and his extensive discography throughout the years, has made him one of the most requested djs in Spain. 

His music has been released by some of the most important labels globally, namely Spinnin’ Records, Universal Music, Sosumi Records, Kryteria Records, Subliminal Records and Pacha Recordings. It has also received support from artists such as David Guetta, Bob Sinclair, Erick Morillo, Carl Cox, Benny Benassi, Sunnery James & Ryan Marciano, Kryder, Fedde Le Grand, Tiesto, Chuckie…

Javi Reina accumulates more than 22 years on the dance floors, more than 100 releases on the market and performances at the best clubs, events and festivals nationally and internationally . He has received 8 awards nationally for best producer, best dj and best record label with rockstar                                                                                                      music. 

He has been a regular name in Ibiza’s scene since his initial years, spinning audience’s favourite house as a resident dj at the island’s main parties like Manumision and SupermartXé at Privilege, Matinee at Space, Blackout at Amnesia, Pacha and This Is Ibiza at Bora Bora.
and recently passed through important clubs and festivals such as EDC (Mexico) BBF (Barcelona) A Summer Story (Madrid) Fabrik Club (Madrid) Ministry of Sound (London) Buena Vida Beach Festival (Cartagena, Colombia)... among many others

This is a summary of Javi Reina’s long career to this day. He is definitely one of the biggest names of house music nationally, present at the most important events. Season after season, he keeps projecting his success in Spain and worldwide.

name: Jengi
bio:
www.jengimusic.com

Mgmt & bookings rana@studiokoto.nl

Jan Theo Berendsen, better known as Jengi, has become a standout name in house music. The Dutch producer and DJ first made waves with his debut single Bruh and the 2017 EP Colors. His vibrant club sound has since propelled him to global recognition.

Jengi's 2023 hit Bel Mercy was a viral sensation, supported by stars like Diplo and Tiësto. The track took TikTok by storm, surpassing 150 million streams, going gold in the Netherlands, sparking a worldwide dance trend and reaching #36 on the Dutch Top 40 with global radio support. Following its success, he released Take U on Tomorrowland Music, amassing over 20 million streams in a year. In 2024, he collaborated with The Magician on Things We Do, a crowd favorite blending their distinct styles.

On stage, Jengi’s rise has been meteoric. He toured Europe, Asia, and Australia in 2023, playing major festivals like Tomorrowland (2023 and 2024). June 2024 marked his debut U.S. tour, further building his international reputation.

Jengi’s remix work, including tracks for Tiësto, Sam Gellaitry, and Mr. Belt & Wezol, alongside releases on Tomorrowland Music, FFRR, and Three Six Zero Records, showcases his versatility and creative vision.

With infectious energy and boundary-pushing music, Jengi is shaping the future of house music.

name: JENNY ANAYA
bio:
Jenny Anaya is a Hardstyle / Rawstyle DJ and producer from Mexico City. From a very young age, her passion for music led her to experiment and learn to play several instruments, including saxophone, drums, guitar, and keyboards, eventually finding her true identity within the most intense and aggressive sounds of electronic music.

In 2018, she made her festival debut at EDC Mexico, considered one of the most important festivals in Latin America, where she has performed on three occasions, reaching the Wasteland stage in 2022.
Thanks to her consistency, passion, and dedication, Jenny achieved her first performance in Europe at Dropzone (Croatia). She has shared the stage with major artists such as Adrenalize, The Purge, Yeyo, GPF,
Bloodlust, Vertile, and Unicorn On Ketamine, performing at events including Dedikted, Loudfriends, Desolate, Aqua Color Fest, Shei and the Gang, Dreamfields Mexico, and Blue Morpho, and will return to
EDC Mexico in 2026.

Jenny now presents her new album “NINJA ZONE”, a conceptual work that represents the evolution of her sound into an intense and unique fusion of Hardstyle, Hard Techno, and Rawstyle. This combination marks
a turning point in her career, consolidating a more industrial and percussive sonic identity.

name: Jerome Isma-Ae
bio:
Jerome Isma-ae combines the behind-the-curtain knowledge of a music producer, with the familiarity and deep knowledge of a dance floor you would expect from a DJ. Broad in his influences, and a visionary in his approach, you’ll always catch a hint of progressiveness in his sound. Jerome has been surrounded by music since his early years, with musical education in elementary school, learning the piano and later the guitar. At 12, Jerome was already playing the bass guitar in a heavy metal band, but it wasn’t until 14 that he started making his own beats with a drum machine.
Having dabbled in many dance music subgenres, you will catch glimpses of Techno, Trance, and Progressive House in his productions. A mean and heavy rhythm section is contrasted against haunting harmonies and counter melodies, and complimented by ambient and textural layers that add a new dimension to the final product. This formula is applied with top-notch clarity and a meticulous attention to detail to such an extent that the sound of his drums has become both recognizable and memorable.

name: Jess Bays
bio:
A testament to her raw passion, ambition and dedication, Jess Bays has fast become one of the standout artists of recent times.

As a broadcaster Jess cut her teeth hosting regular shows on Capital Dance and Rinse FM, later moving on to regularly fill in on across BBC Radio 1, KISS FM and Capital Dance.  
Jess has released a string of hits across labels including Defected, Stress Records, Warner, Polydor, EMI, Armada and Good Company As well as her stand-out original records, Jess has produced remixes for the likes of Marshmello, Rudimental, Becky Hill, Jax Jones and Jazzy. Jess’ undeniable sound has given a fresh narrative to a whole new generation of deeper house music and garnered multiple DSP playlist covers as well as strong support across BBC Radio 1, Kiss & Capital.

Jess has performed right across the globe with shows in Ibiza, Croatia, LA, NYC and Brazil along with highlight sets at Parklife, Tomorrowland, Reading & Leeds, Lakedance, Latitude, Ministry of Sound, Warehouse Project and Printworks.

Jess runs her own highly successful label imprint Superstore Records in partnership with Good Company and with the label’s first release “Temptation” having already amassed over 30M streams, it’s evident that this is a producer and tastemaker with the midas touch.

Jess has certainly proved DJ Mag right, after they tipped her as ‘One to watch’ and with 1.6 million monthly Spotify listeners, a slew of new records at the ready and international tour plans, there’s no stopping this powerhouse – a true artist who’s always evolving, learning, crafting and creating.

name: Jessica Audiffred
bio:
Jessica Audiffred, from the vibrant metropolis of Mexico City, is a trailblazer redefining the landscape of mainstage electronic music. With her unique style, high-energy performances, and boundary-pushing productions, Jessica has become one of the most exciting and influential names in the global dance music scene.

Her rise to prominence has been nothing short of meteoric. Renowned for her electrifying stage presence, Jessica has graced the world’s most iconic festivals, including Tomorrowland, Electric Daisy Carnival, and Lollapalooza. Known for her ability to seamlessly blend multiple genres, she delivers high-energy, fun-filled records in dynamic live sets that are never pre-programmed, creating a one-of-a-kind experience for audiences worldwide.

In 2016, Jessica launched her own label, A Records, as a platform to express her innovative vision and dedication to empowering emerging talent, specifically from Mexico and Latin America, in electronic genres ranging from Bass House to Dubstep. The label has earned widespread recognition, solidifying Jessica as a leader in the electronic music world and beyond. With multiple chart-topping releases and collaborations under her belt, her discography spans partnerships with globally acclaimed artists, while her signature sound continues to evolve and captivate.

Jessica’s unparalleled journey is highlighted by milestone achievements, including headlining a 50-city North America tour, curating Mexico’s first bass music festival to spotlight and uplift local talent, and breaking barriers as one of the few women to dominate the electronic mainstage and headline iconic venues like Hollywood Palladium. Her presence isn’t just felt on the decks but resonates deeply with fans and industry peers who recognize her as a pioneer.

With her highly anticipated debut album, Rave New World, set to release in 2025, Jessica is embarking on an exciting new chapter. Drawing inspiration from her early rave experiences, the album blends her signature festival-ready sound with uplifting, melodic moments,

name: Jim Smith
bio:
Jim Smith is a Texas rapper, he is heavily influenced by rap acts such as Tupac Shakur, Scarface, and South Park Mexican.

Much of his style derives from a deep rooted southern style in English and Spanish lyrics. Smith’s most notable topics are of his ups and downs as a Latino male growing up in a single parent home.

Smith’s fan base often credits him for being able to paint vivid pictures through the art of story telling. The Dallas Observer wrote an article titled "Pearlbase Records Builds a Home For Hispanic Hip Hop in Dallas" which Jim Smith And Dasugarman were interviewed for. Smith's most requested song on Dallas KNON FM radio station is "Hey Girl" from the 80s Baby Album.

Jim Smith is currently working on his own independent label aside from PearlBase to build Corastone Records.


KNOWN ALIASES:  JIM SMITH, and TR3HS.

name: Jimmie Page
bio:
Chicago has been renowned for playing a major part of the birth of underground house music. Embarking on the scene, Jimmie landed rotating guest spots at arguably one of Chicago's first and best underground club SHELTER working with the legendary Chicago promoter Dave Medusa. Jimmie's unique sound of progressive & funky house music, aggression and deck skills, caught the attention of fellow peers. Soon after, the offers started coming in for residencies and guest spots. Jimmie played an integral part of the development of Chicago's progressive scene, which now trail blazes the city's nightlife. With a wave of nightclub openings in Chicago, Jimmie was the top contender to attain his first residency. This was the beginning of a trail for ensuing residencies and countless guest spots in Chicago and abroad. The Club history below says it all. 

In the Dj booth, Jimmie has hosted with the best Djs and Producers the world has to offer. Armin Van Buuren, , Mauro Picotto, Christopher Lawrence, Carl Cox, Chemical Brothers, Max Chapman are only a noted few from an immeasurable list. As the underground music grew, a new trend began. Dance music and mix shows were about to be introduced to the community. Energy's 92.7 & 5 became the catalyst to a full 24/7 dance music format. Jimmie landed one of the most coveted spots in Chicago that of a resident DJ on Chicago's premier dance radio station (WKIE Energy 92.7/5) where he hosted a five hour show called Clubland Friday Night. Jimmie was one of the first to showcase international Djs such as Fatboy Slim, Ferry Corsten, and Christopher Lawrence into a field traditionally held by local talent. Jimmie hasnt forgotten his roots nor his first beginnings. He often makes room for local peers to be a Guest Dj on his show as well. Cultivating an atmosphere of the best known up and coming Dj talent. With the end of an era approaching, Energys 4 year running couldn't handle the pop-culture craze. Somehow Jimmies show had survived. A short time after, Arizonas Dance Station Energy 92.7 & 101.1 had hired a new cutting edge Program Director-Mike Oaks. Immediately being embraced by Mike, Jimmies show had now moved to Phoenix. Taking notice of his resume, the Ultra team had signed him to perform at the Ultra Music Festival 7 in 2005. This accomplished a long time goal. 

With the continued success of his mix show, Jimmie was invited back to perform at Ultra 9. His dedication towards hard work, consistency and perfection was immediately recognized. Passion for his craft and music was an evident formula of the next chapter. The Ultra organization took notice and recruited Jimmie to become a candidate for the position of Host and PD of their upcoming radio show. Through an exhausting list of highly talented prospects, perseverance and devotion made the difference. Jimmie is now has become a huge part of the Ultra tradition as he just completed his 20th Year appearance at Ultra 2024 in Miami. Jimmie also hold a residency at the Infamous Dragon Room Chicago. JP will continue to spread his energy to dance floors and listeners across the world.     WWW.JIMMIEPAGE.COM 

Club History 

	 Past & Current Residencies in Chicago and abroad. 

	The Past, Chicago  - Vision,  GodsKitchen, Spundae, Crobar, Nocturnal, Karma, Rive Gauche, Kustom, Aura, The Drink, Breathe, Transit, Shelter.

	The Past, Abroad  -  Crobar Miami, Kastle - St Louis, Tom Tom’s - Milwaukee, 3 - Milwaukee, Pure - Milwaukee, Fly Bar - Milwaukee, Velvet - Mcallum TX, 

	Current  -  Dragon Room - Ultra Music Festival, Resident DJ of UMF Radio. Appearing at the past 19 UMFs.

	Radio History
PD/Host/DJ of UMF Radio – 3 1/2yrs 
Resident DJ of UMF Radio till present
(XM Channel 80 The Move, The System, Area/Sirus)
PD/Host/DJ of Energy 92.7 & 5s “Clubland Friday Nights” - 3yrs
PD/Host/DJ Arizona’s Dance Station Energy 92.7 & 101 - 2 1/2yrs
CD for Energy 92.7 & 5 – DJ Mix – Sold Out 15,000 copies
Countless Guest Mixes on Stations World Wide

name: Jimmy Page
bio: Jimmy Page, full name James Patrick Page, was born on January 9, 1944 in Heston, Middlesex, England. He is a famous British guitarist, composer and music producer. He was the guitarist, founder and leader of Led Zeppelin, responsible for most of the band's recording and stage production. He started his career as a session musician, became a well-known session guitarist in Britain in the mid-1960s. In 2011, he was ranked third in Rolling Stone's list of the 100 greatest guitarists, won the NME Rock 'n' Roll Soul Award, and was inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame.

name: Jixo & Danz
bio:
- Behind the decks since 2001
- Headlining most of the venues and festivals in UAE and the region.
- Brand ambassadors for Sennheiser Middle East
- Former Resident DJ's B018 Dxb (2022-2024)
- Former resident DJ's at Code, Soho Garden ,Soho Beach Dxb & Hive Dxb (2018-2022)
- Former resident DJ's at Blue Marlin Ibiza UAE ( 2012-2018 ) & Disconekt 
- What's On Nightlife Awards 2022 for Highly Commended resident DJ
- What's On Nightlife Awards 2019 for Best DJ 
- What's On Nightlife Awards 2018 for Best DJ 
- Time Out Dubai Music & Nightlife Awards 2018 for Best DJ
- Hype Nightlife Awards 2017 for Best DJ
- Time Out Dubai Music & Nightlife Awards 2017 for Highly Commended DJ


Playing back-to-back with another DJ is a skill few people have. And we're not talking one-upping the other Dj with tunes they can't follow, we're talking about Djs that feed off each other’s tunes, energy and vibe to create a hands up, heads down affair. Jixo and Danz have made a career out of it, as the two Daniels (Jahed and Azar) work in tandem dropping everything from sunset deep house, Minimal tech, Indie dance, Afro to peak time Melodic and techno.

They've had the pleasure of sharing the decks for DJ heavyweights like Solomun, Black Coffee, Adriatique, Tale of Us, Marco Carola, Micheal Bibi,Jamie Jones, Sven Vath, Luciano, Ame, Carl Cox, Nina Kravitz, Artbat, Maceo Plex, Bedouin, Mochakk and Dixon. Regularly playing to thousands in big festivals and well known underground clubs every weekend such as Terra Solis (Tomorrowland), Elrow, Untold and Ibiza Global Festival, Techno & Chill festival. They’ve also played underground bastions of cool back in Beirut, their home city, including The Basement, Ahm, The Grand Factory, Uberhaus, 2nd Sun, The Garten, Five Island Festival and converted bomb-shelter club, BO18.

Jixo (Daniel Jahed) and Danz (Daniel Azar) have been child hood friends since school, with the tight-knit bond continuing into their first pub, bar and club gigs. Amazingly considering their packed DJ schedule, the DJ duo both work full time in Dubai’s thriving media industry. It hasn’t stopped them from taking international bookings, however, with Jixo & Danz having worked dancefloors as far-flung as Ibiza, Bali, Croatia, Thailand, Malaysia, and Egypt.

Musically, they’ve always positioned themselves at the deeper, groovier end of the house spectrum known for their trademark super-smooth sets, but are equally as comfortable taking it up a notch and dropping a tech-house and techno masterclass when needed. And with their back-to-back energy and peerless selection guaranteed at every gig, Jixo and Danz are proving that two heads (and four hands) is better than one.

name: JK
bio: UZ is an internationally renowned electronic music DJ/producer who has collaborated with major artists like Flosstradamus, Zeds Dead, DJ Shadow, and performed at global music festivals such as Coachella. name: Joachim Garraud
bio:
Legend of electronic music and undisputed showman, Joachim Garraud transports his audience through meticulous productions, combining punchy sounds and elegant melodic arrangements, clever mix of vibrations, frequencies, and pulsations.

During his career, which we celebrate the thirtieth year, he shares his own compositions around the world at unprecedent concerts, but also his knowledge and passion, put at the service of renowned artists; and today with the new generation of producers, notably through his new musical exploration laboratory: the UndGrdMusic label.

Pioneer of the techno movement in the early 90s at the time of the Boy in Paris, this year 2019 marks an inevitable return to the sources and faithful to his first loves.

name: Joe Hardy
bio:
Joe is a songwriter of great depth and variety and is a first class live performer whether solo or with his band. His emphasis on excellence is obvious to everyone. He has been playing stages of all sizes for over 10 years, and is at home in a church hall, a pub or a festival having honed his craft in all three…  

The end of 2016 saw the release of his debut album ‘Without Borders’ - a mixture of acoustic tracks through  to Pop and all out Rock. He has a desire to tell a story and draw others into the process. Without Borders was the result of two years of writing, during this time  Joe penned over 60 songs expressing different parts of his journey  and telling the story of people he was meeting.

Since the release of Without Borders Joe has been leading worship and performing at churches and events all over the UK and into Europe. 2019 sees the release of his new EP ‘Hold Me Close’ - recorded live in a local Church, this is a collection of new and re-arranged songs in an acoustic format. As well as an ever-growing live schedule and further releases.

name: Joezi
bio:
Joezi is a globally Afro-house artist whose music radiates soulful energy and uplifting vibes. Known for his distinctive style that fuses rhythm, emotion, and a touch of magic, Joezi creates immersive soundscapes that resonate deeply with audiences.

With over 60 tracks released on some of the world’s top labels, including the global hit “7 Seconds”—which has amassed over 60 million streams—Joezi’s music is a testament to his talent and passion. His infectious grooves and euphoric beats ignite dance floors, spreading his signature vibes and love for music to every corner of the world.

Born Jhonatan Zirah in Paris in 1987, Joezi infuses his life experiences into his artistry, creating a sound that connects and inspires. Whether in the studio or on stage, Joezi’s music embodies joy, connection, and the power of Afro-house.

name: Johan Gielen
bio:
Johan Gielen is universally recognised as a pioneer and legend of the global trance scene. The word “legend” is at times thrown around too freely these days, but in the world of dance music the Johan Gielen name is engrained deep into the DNA of the trance genre – he’s a founding father of the sound as a producer and DJ, an inspiration to fans and peers alike, and remains a highly energised and influential driving force in the scene today.

When the global trance movement was arguably at the peak of its powers in the 2000’s Johan Gielen ranked as high as #24 in the prestigious DJ Mag Top 100 DJs poll and, to this day, is the only DJ to have performed 15 consecutive times at Trance Energy, the most celebrated of all trance festivals during this era, gaining him the nickname of “Mr Trance Energy”. There’s barely a big stage that hasn’t been graced by Johan’s presence and standout performances over the years – Tomorrowland, Sensation, Dance Valley, Godskitchen, Electronic Family, Mysteryland, Sunrise are just a handful of highlights.

As a producer, Johan Gielen has been creating both club classics and crossover anthems since the dawn of trance music. As a solo artist. the pinnacle was surely his massive ‘Revelations’ album, but he’s also the key member of famed collaboration projects such as Svenson & Gielen (hit singles including ‘The Beauty Of Silence’ / ‘Twisted’) and Airscape (‘Pacific Melody’ / ‘L’Esperanza’). The studio inspiration and output remains as strong as ever today, with late 2016 releases including a remix package of ‘Flash’ and a new Airscape single ‘Right Here’ featuring Betsie Larkin on vocals.

During the 25 year career of Johan Gielen countless artists have risen and fallen, yet very few have stayed the course . The secret to Johan Gielen’s enduring success is his commitment and consistency within the scene he loves. He was adorned with the nickname “Mr Trance Energy” by his fans and he’s stayed true to the genre he loves – the Johan Gielen name is synonymous with trance and he’s mighty proud of it!

There’s no better artist to deliver a Trance Classics set than Johan Gielen and he’s delighted to deliver the sounds that the fans demand – the Johan Gielen Trance Classics concept is booming in clubland and festival stages are planned for 2017. Johan also delivers Volume 3 of his popular Trance Classics compilation series on the High Contrast label this October. Moving into 2017 there will be further new productions with the Johan Gielen name being used to deliver the classic trance sounds, while his Airscape alter ego will be cutting a new edge to the trance scene that Johan has been shaping for over two decades now.

Johan Gielen is Trance – “Mr Trance Energy”!

During his carrer he'd produced amazing tracks which support has reached new horizons over 125 countries until now signed by Enormous Tunes (Sirup Music), Future Rave Music, Intensity Records, Trippy Code Records, supported and played by David Guetta, Armin van Buuren Radio  'A State Of Trance'  by Steve Dekay, B Jones, Rebūke and so on; playlisted by Spinnin' Records, Revealed Recordings, Beatport Best New Mainstage, David Guetta Playlist and so forth. John is constantly improving himself in many ways, fighting for one special place within each heart in the earth through his unique style, and this might only be the beginning of something much bigger.

name: John Bowtie
bio:
Let’s start at the beginning. My dad was a DJ in nightclubs, and I grew up immersed in music of every kind. One of my earliest memories was digging through his CD collection, picking albums based solely on the artwork. That’s how I discovered Conspiracy of One by The Offspring—you know, the one with the flaming skull and crazy colors. That album cracked open my teenage rock phase and pushed me to start playing drums at 15. I ended up playing in rock bands until my early twenties.
That foundation eventually led me to my true home—electronic music.

As a DJ, I see myself as a storyteller. I’m a disciple of Laurent Garnier, in spirit. Every set I play is tailor-made for the night, the space, and most importantly, the crowd. I don’t pre-pack sets—I feel it out live, making every show a one-off journey.
Musically, I orbit around House—with a capital H. From deep and minimal to tech, classic, UK Garage, or even hard house—I love creating sets with variety and edge. I also enjoy slipping in the unexpected, giving people something new to take home with them.
I’m a bit of a nerd when it comes to sound. I obsess over acoustics, lighting, and layout, and I apply that same precision to my productions. Everything I create is built for the dance-floor, with a focus on sound design, groove, and that physical, gut-level connection. My latest work lives between House, Power House, and Tech House—music with a mature, distinct tone made to stand the test of time.

Some collabs I’ve loved: working with Moojo on Calamar Records, and more recently a release on AZZUR Music (Meloko’s label) with N-You-Up.

name: Jon Batiste
bio:
In 2020, Jon Batiste redefined what it means to be productive. 
Recording and releasing two Grammy-nominated albums, check. 
Cultural and social advocacy, check. Band leading and music directing on the Late Show, check. Practicing, learning, sharing, growing, check.
 
Was he done there? Batiste? Nah. 
 
He decided to build a studio in his dressing room at the renowned Ed Sullivan Theater and invited a few talented friends—including singer-songwriter Autumn Rowe and producer Kizzo—to lay the blueprint for what is destined to become a seminal masterpiece of black pop American music, his forthcoming album, WE ARE. Not to mention, he put all of this together in 6 days. Again, redefining productivity. 
 
He ended the year with his music and likeness being centrally featured in the Disney/Pixar film Soul. His composing and songwriting will be featured in his large-scale, genre-melding symphonic work “American Symphony,” set to premiere at Carnegie Hall in 2021 and he has been developing a broadway musical about the life of famed painter Jean-Michel Basquiat.
 
Jon balances a demanding performance schedule—which often includes his signature ‘love riot’ street parades—with public speaking engagements, masterclasses, brand partnerships, community activism, and acting roles. In the wake of George Floyd's murder and in the lead up to the election, he organized several peaceful protest marches, leading thousands in the streets of New York City. He played himself on the HBO series Tremé and appeared in Spike Lee’s Red Hook Summer and Da Sweet Blood of Jesus.

Every story has a name. Jon Connor evokes transmissions of emotions through layered textures and the soaring sounds of nostalgia. As the reality you know, he distorts and disintegrates, and he takes you on a journey between space, time, and your desires, Dissolved into sonic bliss.
Renowned for worldwide hits such as Sunshine Daydream, Acid Drop, and Taipei Awakening, Jon Connor's ever-changing sound is a testament to his innovative trance infusions Acid & techno. 

His unique techno style, constantly evolving, redefines the new-age techno scene, showcasing his creativity and originality. 

Jon Connor, a European national hailing from Wales, Uk, currently residing in Taiwan, is a fantastic pure techno-producing talent. 
Connor has been a producer & DJ for over a decade, carrying his musical message of emotionally uplifting techno to every corner of the world, from huge festivals Like ULTRA to the most prominent urban clubs & underground events. 

Devoted to his art and a true perfectionist, Jon Connor is known for his massive hypnotic explosive sets on the dancefloor & his precise, detailed production in the studio. Both are always laced with tons of feel-good musicality. 

With countless DJ gigs worldwide that he has already played and his outstanding productions, you can always find him in the top rows of the sales charts. His past and new releases can be seen regularly in the DJ sets by Nina Kraviz, Amelie Lens, Farrago, Kobosil, Daxj, 999999999, Reinier Zonneveld, Adam Beyer Klangkünstler, Deborah de Luca, and many more regarding driving techno, Jon is one of the first addresses. 

His influence on the techno and Acid trance music community is undeniable, and his story leaves ego and arrogance in the cloakroom. 

It places the focus where it should be on music.

name: Jordan Arts
bio:
Jordan Arts is a South African born electronic music producer, vocalist and multi-instrumentalist based in London. 
Since performing his debut live performance in Amsterdam in 2021, Jordan has been the official opening act for some of the biggest electronic music acts in the world, such as Black Coffee, Joris Voorn, Nora en Pure, ARTBAT, Claptone and Cosmic Gate. 

In 2022 Jordan was featured in the August edition of Mixmag Caribbean for his undeniable talent and knowledge in electronic music. 
Later in the year he was featured by Beatport as their “Artist To Look Out For” and wrote about his sensational live show. 
His collaboration with Da Africa Deep, “Need to Know” featured on Black Coffees tribute album for Virgil Abloh.

Jordan is soon to open for ARTBAT in Brazil for the famous Rio Carnival. 
We look forward to watching his journey unfold, allowing us the continued opportunity to connect deeper with ourselves through his music.

name: Jorn Pricez
bio:
Jorn Pricez is a Belgian DJ and producer with a powerful and energetic sound. His music is built around a style he calls “Trancy Rave,” where trance-inspired elements come together with the drive of modern techno.

As a child, Jorn was inspired by trance music and the sounds of that era. These influences are still clearly present in his work today. Rather than simply repeating the past, he reworks classic trance elements into a sound that fits modern dancefloors.

Alongside his own releases, Jorn shares his creative process and production techniques online. He is also an instructor at the Tomorrowland Academy, where he works closely with artists from different backgrounds and helps them shape their sound.

His journey has brought him to beautiful stages and club settings across Europe, including Tomorrowland Belgium & Winter, Castle of Love in the Netherlands, and Club Di Mare in Spain. He is also the founder of the event concept Rebiza, where his musical vision comes to life.

name: JUAN (AR)
bio:
Juan (AR), is an Argentinian to the core, born and raised. He’s a DJ and Producer in the well-known underground scene of Buenos Aires. His style is way more unique than his name, if you know what we mean… It sways from groovy Minimal / Deep Tech to thumping Tech House featuring powerful bass lines and crisp percussions. 

Not only he plays chill beats, but he has reached the Beatport Top 10 many times. His trajectory doesn’t end there though… he was signed by leading labels of the industry like Deeperfect, Circus Recordings, Clarisse and Lost.

Juan (AR) has received a tremendous amount of support from popular international DJs such as Marco Carola, Jamie Jones, Joseph Capriati, The Martinez Brothers, Archie Hamilton, wAFF, Franky Rizardo, Nicole Moudaber and more top artists in the best clubs and parties worldwide like Music On, Amnesia, Ants and Creamfields.

Let’s talk clubs now, Juan (AR) has played in the finest clubs across South America like Creamfields, Crobar, Rio Electronic Music, The Bow, La Fabrica, Pacha, Palacio Alsina, Savage, La Playa, 4get and Club69 (Argentina), he toured across Spain, Andorra, Brasil, Costa Rica, Ecuador, Chile, Paraguay, Peru and Uruguay. He has also played alongside top artists like Marco Carola, Dennis Cruz, Franky Rizardo, Ale de Tuglie, Solardo, Richy Ahmed, Hector Couto, Claptone, Rafa Barrios, Frank Storm, Melanie Ribbe, Domenico Scavone, Lexlay and Bassel Darwish.

name: Juan Soto
bio:
Felizmente: Coyoacano, Músico, Productor, Cantante y Letrista. 

JUAN SOTO — Arquitecto de un sonido disco con
tintes electrónicos de una personalidad única  y alquimista de la pista de baile.
Desde el corazón palpitante de la Ciudad de México surge Juan Soto, un visionario de la música electrónica que ha hecho de la experimentación su bandera y del ritmo su lenguaje universal. Productor, cantante, DJ, letrista, compositor, locutor, y maestro, Soto lleva desde 2006 moldeando el pulso sonoro de una generación, desdibujando fronteras entre géneros, estilos y escenas.
Lo que comenzó como un acto de exploración independiente lo ha llevado a convertirse en una figura clave de la electrónica mexicana contemporánea. Ha dejado su huella como parte del dúo Moon Runner, la ambiciosa banda Supercontinent y el hipnótico y cautivante proyecto Destino Sonido, al lado de la joven promesa Labibe. Sus colaboraciones revelan su visión sin etiquetas: ha compartido créditos con nombres como Bufi, Dan Solo, Technicolor Fabrics, Future Feelings, Trans X, Zemmoa y Nomi Ruiz, entre otros, formando un universo sonoro en constante expansión.
Apasionado por el arte de compartir música, Juan Soto ha hecho vibrar las pistas de baile más emblemáticas de la capital mexicana —como el antiguo Club Social Rhodesia ahora Bar Oriente, M.N. Roy y Departamento Studio Bar— y ha traspasado fronteras con sets en espacios de culto como el mítico Hotel Costes en París y Moog en Barcelona. Cada presentación suya es un ritual sonoro, una narrativa electrónica donde convergen lo íntimo, lo experimental y lo eufórico.
No conforme con transformar escenarios, Soto también ha cultivado mentes. En 2014 fundó Karate Audio, academia pionera en producción musical independiente, que ha dado vida a más de 20 generaciones de artistas, entre los que destacan Wet Baes, Pahua, Paurro y Fausto. Su labor como mentor ha sido clave en el surgimiento de una nueva ola de talento latinoamericano que hoy resuena a nivel global.
Ha compartido cartel con figuras internacionales en festivales como EDC, Bahidorá, Ceremonia, Vive Latino, Trópico y hasta la Feria del Libro Infantil, donde sorprendió con una re-musicalización en vivo de The Cameraman (1928), homenajeando a Buster Keaton con una sensibilidad contemporánea que conecta el cine mudo con la electrónica del siglo XXI.
Juan Soto no sigue tendencias: las crea, las deconstruye y las reinterpreta. Su carrera es una invitación constante a escuchar con otros oídos, a mover el cuerpo y despertar la mente. Siempre en movimiento, siempre en evolución, es un artista que no teme explorar los márgenes del sonido para, desde ahí, reinventar el centro.

name: julie marghilano
bio:
Julie Marghilano is a multifaceted musician, performer, and educator—a classically trained violinist, daughter of a jazz musician father and vocalist mother, and a true embodiment of musical versatility. Her deep-rooted passion and unwavering dedication have shaped a career that spans genres and pushes boundaries.
As a DJ, producer, and founder of Sol Asylum Records, Julie is known for her eclectic selections, drawing from an expansive record library that explores the nuances of dance music and beyond.
Julie has performed at iconic festivals and venues worldwide, including Honcho (Pittsburgh), Golden Records (New York), Enter (Japan), and Freerotation (UK).
Her latest release on Rawax further cements her place in the underground music scene, while her ever expanding Track ID YouTube channel reflects her deep commitment to curating and sharing the finest vinyl records. Julie is a passionate advocate for inclusivity in music, rejecting the notion of gatekeeping and instead dedicating her life to sharing knowledge and music with others.
In addition to her DJing and production work, Julie leads the Sonic Foundation workshop, where she mentors aspiring artists in music production and sound design. Her innovative spirit shines in experimental soundscapes, blending violin, FX, and loop machines—a style showcased on the Advanced Public Listening compilation alongside luminaries like Matthew Herbert and Ricardo Villalobos.
Through her captivating performances, boundary-pushing productions, and educational initiatives, Julie Marghilano continues to shape and inspire the global music community, bridging genres and creating moments of unity through sound.

name: Jupiter & Okwess
bio:
Jupiter Bokondji was born in Kinshasa 48 years ago, into a family of griots from the Mongo region. His grandmother, a reknowned healer, introduced him to traditional rhythm and music including the famous Zebola from Ekonda (Mongo région of the equator), rythms that are said to cure the sick. From a young age, Jupiter played percussion instruments and acommpanied his grandmother to funerals and weddings. However, it was in Germany East-Berlin, where he spent his adolescence, and where his father was appointed executive assistant for the Congos embassy in 1974, that he discovered Europe and it’s vibrant music scene – the Stones, Deep Purple, James Brown etc. Here, he set up his own rock band called ‘’Der Neger’’ with fellow young Berliners; their sound was a strange cocktail of Mongo percussions and guitar Zeppelin-esque guitar. The band was successful in all the shady clubs in town. But, his father’s mandate ended. 

When he was 20, Jupiter went back in the eighties bubbling Kinshasa head full of dreams, glory and sounds unimaginable to most of his friends. He left the family home, earning a living singing at funerals and playing percussion in several local orchestras.

With his earnings he travelled the Congo, searching for new sounds. These travels reinforced his feeling that all the music he heard in Europe originated from the 450 ethnic groups that made up his country, and that the tribalism that tore his country apart politically, could in fact become a unifying force.

He continued to develop his own unique style, surrounding himself with trendy musicians connected to from Europe. His idea was to reactivate the forgotten rhythms and melodies of Congo, by injecting the urban groove of Kinhasa. He called this explosive mix ‘’ Bofenia Rock’ and in 1983, succeeded in forming his first orchestra, Bongofolk, who became his musical experiment.

In 1990, he created Okwess International, a group which contained within it avant-garde artists such as Jean Goubald. This group travelled Africa, and was selected to represent Zaire in 1999 at MASA (Market of African Performing Arts). Just at the point where their luck seemed to be finally turning, the country fell into chaos due to civil war.

In early 2004, Jupiter met Renaud Barret and Florent de la Tullaye. The connection was immediate, so much so that the two French returned to record the songs of Okwess International, and other groups surrounding Jupiter, such as Staff Benda Billili.

Of this time, Jupiter says ‘ I knew something like this would happen. I was convinced of it.’’

‘The Dance of Jupiter,’ a film documenting his musical exploration was released in 2007. On screen, we see his slender silhoutte exploring the various districts of Kinhasa, discovering talent artists undiscovered and unkown by the rest of the world. Little Jupiters, he calls them.

“Today, we find many young groups conitnuing in my footsteps, dipping into our archives for inspiration; my mission is complete. If I disappeared today, I will have achieved my goal’’

Of course, Jupiter wasn’t about to disappear; he is poised for a fantastic career. In October 2011, he played Kinhasa to an audience of five thousand; five thousand people hypnotised by the powerful rhythm and energy of Okwess International. In July of the same year, he was part of the the album created by Damon Albarn, of Blur and Gorillaz fame, ‘Kinhasa One Two,’ released in November 2011 through WARP.

His album ‘Hotel Universe’ will be released in 2012, and will undoubtedly give him the recognition that he deserves.

name: Justin Pollnik
bio:
DJ/Producer from Leipzig, Germany

Justin Pollnik ist erst 26 Jahre alt, doch schon international mit seiner einzigartigen Live-Show unterwegs. Moderner Hardstyle trifft auf EDM, Hardtechno, Rawstyle & Uptempo - eine europaweit einzigartige Kombination. Diese konnte er bereits auf dem Ultra Music Festival, WORLD CLUB DOME, Airbeat One Festival, Sputnik Spring Break, BREAK the RULES Festival, Electrisize oder Open Beatz Festival beweisen.

Internationale Headline-Shows in Thailand, Kroatien, Malta oder auf Ibiza sowie eine sogar in der ARD Sportschau gezeigte Show vor über 25.000 Gästen bei der offiziellen DFB-Pokalfeier von RB Leipzig reihen sich in über 100 gespielte Bookings im Jahr 2023 ein.

Seine millionenfach gestreamten Top-Hits "Ostdeutschland" und "Der Bass" oder das weltweit virale Cover des Klassikers "Major Tom" bescherten Justin Pollnik bereits mehr als 10 Millionen Spotify-Streams. Mit dem offiziellen Remix des Hits "Es Eskaliert" ist der Leipziger zum erfolgreichsten Hardstyle-Act Deutschlands aufgestiegen.

Mit seinem Song "Skyline" konnte der DJ und Produzent den German Songwriting Award 2023 gewinnen, was er seinen über 100.000 Followern auf TikTok und 35.000 Followern auf Instagram stolz präsentieren konnte.

name: Justus
bio:
Amsterdam-born producer and DJ Justus is pushing the edges of techno and house, taking every crowd on a ride.

Justus has made his mark with standout releases like You Are My X, 909, Push Me, and Dance To The Beat, plus collaborations such as Motivated and Dance With Me with Kaskade, Make It Count with Armin van Buuren, and an official remix for David Guetta & Sia. His remix of Benny Benassi’s Satisfaction became a global moment after Gordo premiered it at Tomorrowland, amassing over 300 million views. 

Launching his own label Just Records, Justus continues to showcase his versatile signature sound. Supported by Calvin Harris, Swedish House Mafia, Diplo, and more, he is firmly cemented among the top emerging names shaping the future of electronic music. He has performed on the world’s biggest stages, including Tomorrowland, Ultra, Ushuaïa and many more. 

Justus is definitely an artist you should have on your radar.

name: Juwlz
bio:
Juwlz is a melodic progressive DJ and composer, known for emotionally driven sets that move between depth and uplift. Her sound is guided by feeling rather than trends or hype. She builds her sets with a strong sense of flow, moving from deep, grounding grooves to high energy moments.

Her debut track, Galactic Call, is already out, marking the first step in a series of upcoming releases.

She has performed at well-known venues and festivals across Belgium and the Netherlands, including Kompass (Ghent), Fuse (Brussels), Extrema Outdoor, Tomorrowland, Club Vaag (Antwerp), Hartstocht festival (NL), Lief festival (NL), as well as numerous other major and underground events.

After her performances, the reactions are often the same: “So young, yet such a refined music selection,” “Her energy is contagious,” and “Her set felt healing.” It’s this combination of musical intuition, emotional depth, and clear artistic vision that sets Juwlz apart behind the decks.

name: K1
bio:
Helping to put Derby on the map; UK rap riser K1 is ready to shake up the music industry!

Born and raised in the East Midlands city of Derby, K1 has already made quite the impression on the music industry. Journeying back to his viral hit ‘Broken Hearted Crook’ back in October of 2021, which has since gained over 8 million streams and views combined. The UK rap riser has gained a growing following on both TikTok (35k+) and Instagram (29k+) not to mention his 900,000+ monthly listeners on Spotify, that are eager to hear what’s next from the artist.

Standing as one of the many up-and-coming artists that are holding it down for the UK’s regional talent outside of the capital, K1 grew up in a small city named Derby nestled in the East Midlands. “Growing up in Derby was alright. The area I grew up in was in between the rough area and the nice area. I used to play a lot of football and sing a lot – I’ve been quite musical from day one”, K1 explained, “The only problem with Derby when I was growing up was not everyone was on the same length with music in comparison to somewhere like London. Derby was a bit shy of that!”.
In between playing football with his friends and competing in the school talent shows as a singer, K1 grew up on an assortment of genres, “I listened to a lot of Eminem and N Dubz growing up! *laughs* I listen to quite a lot of genres; Lo-Fi, Rap, Alternative, and Indie music, it’s never been just one sound” he said. Although admitting that his vocals weren’t always the strongest at school, music has always been one of K1’s first interests…little did he know that in the years to follow it would change his life.

Back in 2018 he would visit his friend’s house who had a small studio set-up that they would play around with; switching the vocals for bars, K1 made a freestyle to Headie One’s track ‘Both’ that received further encouragement from his friends to start writing raps instead. “I sacked the singing off and started rapping about everything. I started getting into Drill, but I think that’s because that was what was popping at the time. I thought to myself am I really a Drill artist like that? Is that what I really want to talk about? My whole persona just changed from then. I wanted to talk about things that were relevant to me as opposed to the streets; I was getting sick of hearing that!”, K1 explained.

Dropping his debut single back in 2021 named ‘Demon’ he followed through with a string of singles that aligned with Drill. However, after a moment of reflection and realising that this wasn’t a true representation of his artistry he released ‘Broken Hearted Crook’ as a freestyle on TikTok and it flew! Gaining positive feedback from people all over the world who could relate to the track he released it as an official single, “I didn’t even know how to use TikTok – I wasn’t even going to drop the track! *laughs* It was just a freestyle, and it got a mad reaction. Prior to that song I had already written a lot of songs like that so for it to get that reaction I thought this must be my way to get recognition in music properly.”

Sustaining the buzz around his name he swiftly followed through with ‘Why You Gotta Lie’. Having the same effect as his previous single, ‘Why You Gotta Lie’ broadened his fanbase further and created yet another viral moment. With people quick to compare him to UK hit-maker Central Cee, K1 took it in his stride to steer away from being labelled a “TikTok sample rapper” with his next release and drop something closer to home, “My music can’t be anymore relatable to me; it can frustrate me at times, but I do take it as a compliment because Central Cee is huge and such a good artist. But I am not trying to give off the persona or do the same thing”, he explained. “‘Derby to Moston’ I made before ‘Broken Hearted Crook’ in fact I even made the video for that before I released the track. I was going to drop another song with an Akon sample, but I wanted to drop something that felt real before I get labelled as a TikTok sample rapper. ‘Derby to Moston’ means a lot, it’s a very personal song to me.”

Like any artist who’s a perfectionist with their craft, K1 prefers to go to the studio and record several tracks at a time, “I’ll write a couple of songs before I go to the studio; I don’t tend to write in the studio that much unless I’m going in with a producer and we’ll whip something up from scratch. Normally I like to take my time, do it from home and really put some effort into it. When I get to the studio, I’ll lay them out, I usually do a few songs per session; even if I don’t like all of them, I’ll know at least I’ve made one hit today. I’m a perfectionist!”.

Gearing up for his latest single ‘Different Man from the project “Love Never Dies”, that is set to drop on February 17th, K1 delivers a thought-provoking track that explores a relationship gone south,
“I don’t know how to feel no more
I kick down the door but I still feel raw
Cause I’m fucked right down to the core
But you would be too if you saw what I saw
Pain in my heart that I can’t ignore”

Expressing that music is his source of therapy he unravels a vulnerable side to himself that enables fans to feel resonate with his artistry further. Underpinned with a spacious and acoustic-led beat he seamlessly glosses the angelic sample that instantly leaves you wanting more.
With a string of successful singles behind him, K1 has been working hard to create a body of work to showcase not only his artistry but his versatility too, “I’ve finished my album now which will be coming out next year; a lot of the tracks on there are real rap and lo-fi. I’ve also got tracks that are more melodic and others that incorporate Drill, so I am trying to branch out”, he commented. Only touching the surface of what he’s capable of, the Derby artist is one to watch this year!

name: Kandarta
bio:
Osamu M and Hideo Kobayashi have formed a neo-techno unit that masterfully expresses an emotional, storytelling, cinematic, and spectacular world through their music akin to watching an epic film.

They have released three tracks, including "Moon Illusion", a piece characterized by its powerful beats. The synthesizer loops gradually evolve and fuse, culminating in the subtle sadness of the dramatic latter half. This track is brimming with originality.

In 2023, they launched their own event, "NEURON" at Womb in Tokyo, inviting acclaimed artists such as Mathame, Hunter/Game, SOEL, and Yubik. The overwhelming support from both the guest performers and the enthusiastic crowd, combined with Kandarta's captivating performance, ignited a new movement and marked a tremendous success.

In June 2024, Kandarta made waves with their performance at the world-renowned and historic club Amnesia Ibiza for the event “NEO”, hosted by Mathame and starring The Blaze and Lyke. In September, they took the stage at the fully packed Ultra Japan RESISTANCE After Party, delivering an electrifying performance alongside Massano and Miss Monique, leaving the crowd thrilled. Furthermore, the highly praised performance in June led to Kandarta being invited back as a special guest for the “NEO” closing party at Amnesia Ibiza. Their B2B set with Mathame became a stunning highlight, marking a monumental achievement for a Japanese artist.

Kandarta's rising fame is rapidly gaining recognition in the global top-tier scene, and their future developments are generating even greater anticipation.

'House and Techno with influences of wildlife.'

Hailing from the Amazone riverbanks in South-America but stationed in the South of the Netherlands: meet Kapibara. The brothers are known to infuse their cultural heritage into their music and shows.
Kapibara aims at a wide array of fascinating sounds: sometimes dark and deep, sometimes colorful and ecstatic, but always a harmonic blending of storytelling percussion power and melodic madness. 
Kapibara’s signature sound floats somewhere between melodic / afro / house and techno. Converging feeling and emotion into a pallet of natural grooves. These chameleons bring great adaptability and they are fully attuned to each other when playing their b2b sets.

With releases on Renaissance, Frau Blau, Eklektisch, Ritter Butzke, Connected Frontline, Abracadabra, Mago Music, Cacao, Bar 25, Atmosphere Records and Sincopat they’ll guide you on an expedition in the electronic spectrum.

bio:
KARABA is a DJ and producer blending her roots as a professional dancer with deep, hypnotic club sounds rooted in Afro house. Born in France and raised in Montréal, she toured the world as a dancer with major global artists before shifting fully into DJing and production in 2016.

Her genre-fluid sets have taken her across North America, Europe, Africa, and Asia, earning a reputation for powerful, immersive performances. With a 2024 Juno nomination and a 2026 FEMMY Awards nomination for Album or EP of the Year (TIDES Remixes), KARABA continues to explore darker, emotional, and rhythm-driven dance floors worldwide.

bio:

After an impressive year that included appearances on The Library Stage at Tomorrowland and The Towers at Rock Werchter, Karakals continues to strengthen her position within the DJ scene. With her infectious energy during her performances, Karakals knows how to make any event truly explode. Her sets, filled with commercial hits, energetic edits and original bootlegs, deliver an unforgettable performance that appeals to every target audience. 

Alongside her recent appearances at Ostend Beach Festival, Sunrise Festival and Hype'O Dream, among others, Karakals previously built up international experience with shows at Balaton Sound in Hungary, Tropics in Spain and multiple shows in Switzerland. The coming year promises to be a year full of new adventures, surprising releases of original tracks and performances you won't want to miss!

name: Karol G
bio:
Karol G was born in Medellin, Colombian on February 14th. Her musical career began at an early age when she participated in school competitions and music festivals.

At the age of 14, while still in high school, she decided to participate in X Factor; it was on that reality show where she made her first appearance in the music industry.

Karol G studied music at la Universidad de Antioquia in Medellin and worked on her vocal techniques. She traveled to the United States to specialize as an entrepreneur in the music field and to study English. 

She returned to Colombia and began working as an independent artist until 2010 when Reykon “El Líder” offered her the opportunity to join him on his world tour. In 2012 Karol G achieved her first number one hit worldwide with the song “301” which sparked tours in countries like: Venezuela, Uruguay, Panama, United States, Puerto Rico, Aruba, Ecuador, Paraguay and Colombia.  With the video for “301” Karol G got to number one on HTV.

Her next single was “Amor de dos”, a song that she recorded alongside Puerto Rican artist, Nicky Jam. Again, achieving the one number spot on radio that she longed for. After achieving two hit songs, “301” and “Amor de dos”, she decided to release a single called “Ricos Besos”, a mixture of innocence and sensuality, a song that is still played in Colombia and throughout other countries.

She ended 2014 with two successful hits, which caught the attention of the public for their unique sound. “Mañana”, alongside Colombian artist Andy Rivera, reaching number one in radio stations throughout Colombia and in countries like Peru and Spain. 

The second single “Si te Confieso,” was pushed solely through digital platforms, achieving great results reaching over 200,000 views on Youtube within the first day of release. “Si te Confieso,” reached great success without releasing into radio.
